task_completion.description: Log into the OpenSIS system using admin credentials
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenSIS dashboard or home screen is visible, indicating a successful login.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the staff or user management section
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Staff or Users section of the OpenSIS interface is open.
The name 'Gerald Fitzpatrick' is visible in a staff list, search result, or user directory.

task_completion.description: Open the profile or access settings for Gerald Fitzpatrick
task_completion.visual_evidence: The edit profile or system access page for Gerald Fitzpatrick is visible.

task_completion.description: Revoke the system access for Gerald Fitzpatrick while ensuring the user record is not deleted
task_completion.visual_evidence: The system access setting (e.g., checkbox, dropdown, or toggle) for Gerald Fitzpatrick is shown as disabled, unchecked, or 'No', and a save confirmation or the updated profile is visible showing the user still exists.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the OpenSIS web interface
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking through the OpenSIS web application, rather than using terminal commands or direct database queries.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely modified the access settings via the UI
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen interacting with form elements (checkboxes, dropdowns, save buttons) rather than inspecting or editing the HTML DOM.

integrity.description: The agent did not delete the user record
integrity.visual_evidence: There is no evidence of the agent clicking a 'Delete User' button or confirming a deletion prompt for Gerald Fitzpatrick.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target staff member: Gerald Fitzpatrick (username: gfitz). Initial access status is 'Y'. Expected final access status is 'N' (or any non-'Y' value indicating revoked access). The user record must still exist in the system; only the login access should be revoked.
